subject_tmpl: Streetlight out on {STREET} near {CROSS}
body_tmpl: Hi, I wanted to report that the streetlight at the corner of {STREET} and {CROSS} has been dark for at least {DAYS} days now. It's a real safety concern — that intersection gets pretty dark after sunset and there's no other light nearby. I've almost missed seeing pedestrians twice this week. Can someone please look into getting it repaired? My name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I live at {ADDRESS}. Thanks for any help.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for reporting this, {FIRST}. We've logged a repair order for the light at {STREET} and {CROSS} and have notified ComEd. Typical turnaround is 5–10 business days once they receive our ticket. We'll follow up if there are delays.

subject_tmpl: Graffiti on the retaining wall at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, there is spray-painted graffiti covering most of the retaining wall on {STREET} just south of {CROSS}. It appeared sometime over the weekend and it's pretty offensive — I'd rather not describe it in an email. I have kids who walk past there every morning. Is there a Village crew that handles graffiti removal, or do I need to contact someone else? — {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}

subject_tmpl: Noisy neighbor at {ADDRESS} — music until 2am
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here at {ADDRESS}. My next-door neighbor has been playing loud music and hosting what sounds like large gatherings until well past 2:00 in the morning on weeknights. I've politely asked them to keep it down twice but nothing has changed. Last night was the worst yet — the bass was shaking my walls. Is there a noise ordinance I can reference, and can a code officer or officer respond after midnight when this is actually happening? I work early shifts and this is affecting my health.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, we've created a complaint record for {ADDRESS}. Our code division coordinates with the Police Department on after-hours noise. An officer can be dispatched during an active disturbance — call the non-emergency line at any time when the noise is occurring so we can document it in real time. Your case number is {CASE}.

subject_tmpl: Barking dog — {ADDRESS}, ongoing problem
body_tmpl: I'm writing about a dog at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. The animal barks for hours at a stretch, often starting before 6 a.m. This has been going on for about {DAYS} days. I don't know the owner's name but I can point out the exact property. I've tried calling the non-emergency police line twice and nothing seems to have changed. Is there a formal complaint process through your office? I'm happy to provide a written statement if needed. — {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Still no response from the Village — case {CASE}
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I submitted a complaint {DAYS} days ago (case {CASE}) about the streetlight outage on {STREET} and I have received absolutely no response — not even an acknowledgment. I followed up by phone on {DATE} and was told someone would call me back. Still nothing. I pay property taxes in this village and I think I deserve at least a status update. Can you personally look into why this is falling through the cracks? My number is {PHONE}. — {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, I sincerely apologize for the lack of communication. I've personally flagged case {CASE} and asked my assistant to get you a status update by end of day. This is not the standard of service I expect from our office and I appreciate you holding us accountable.

subject_tmpl: Multiple streetlights out on {STREET} between {CROSS} and {NUM}
body_tmpl: There are now three consecutive streetlights out on {STREET} between {CROSS} and the {NUM} block. The stretch is completely dark at night and there have been a couple of near-accidents that I've personally witnessed. I drive this route every day. I reported one of them about two weeks ago and it still hasn't been fixed — now two more are out. Please escalate this. {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, thank you for the heads-up on the cluster outage. We've opened a priority work order and will do a full sweep of {STREET} this week. We appreciate your patience.

subject_tmpl: Graffiti on Metra underpass — {STREET}
body_tmpl: Dear Mayor Okonkwo — the Metra underpass on {STREET} near the UP-W station has had the same graffiti tags on it for over a month. I understand the underpass structure may be Metra's property but the area reflects on Bellwood. Can the Village reach out to Metra or at least coordinate a clean-up? Visitors coming off the train see this first thing. Residents deserve better. Sincerely, {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Loud party at {ADDRESS} every weekend
body_tmpl: Officer Banks or whoever handles this — the house at {ADDRESS} on {STREET} throws loud outdoor parties most Friday and Saturday nights. This past {DATE} it went until nearly 3 a.m. I've called non-emergency twice. Officers have come out and things quiet down for an hour and then it's right back to full volume. Is there anything more permanent the Village can do? I'm losing sleep and so is my {AGE}-year-old. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}
reply_tmpl: Thank you for the report, {FIRST}. I've flagged this address for enhanced patrol on weekend nights. We'll coordinate with Code Enforcement for a daytime follow-up as well. Please keep calling non-emergency when it's actively happening — each call creates a log entry that strengthens any future enforcement action.

subject_tmpl: Dissatisfied with code enforcement response — {CASE}
body_tmpl: I'm writing to express my frustration with how case {CASE} was handled. I reported an overgrown, junk-filled yard at {ADDRESS} on {DATE}. A code officer apparently visited but issued only a verbal warning. Three weeks later the property looks exactly the same. Verbal warnings clearly aren't working. I'd like to know what the next step is and when a written notice will be issued. I'm not trying to be difficult — I just want the neighborhood to look decent. {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: General dissatisfaction — Village responsiveness
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I've been a Bellwood resident for {DAYS} years and I've noticed a real decline in how quickly the Village responds to everyday concerns. Lights stay out for weeks, complaints go unanswered, and when you do reach someone you get a vague non-answer. I'm not asking for perfection, just basic responsiveness. I hope you'll look at how calls and emails are tracked and make some improvements. A concerned resident, {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}.
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, thank you for this candid feedback. Responsiveness is something I take personally. I've shared your note with our department heads and we are reviewing how complaints are tracked and closed. I welcome a follow-up conversation — please call my office at Village Hall if you'd like to talk directly.

subject_tmpl: Streetlight on {STREET} flickering dangerously
body_tmpl: The light on {STREET} right in front of {ADDRESS} has been flickering on and off for {DAYS} days. Not totally out — it strobes, actually, which in some ways is worse because it's distracting to drivers. I worry about someone having a seizure or causing an accident from the distraction. Please treat this as urgent. My name is {FIRST} {LAST} and you can reach me at {PHONE} if you need more information about the exact pole.

subject_tmpl: Barking dog also seems neglected — {STREET}
body_tmpl: I want to flag a situation on {STREET} near {ADDRESS}. There's a dog that barks virtually all day, every day. When I walk past I can see it's kept outside in all weather with what looks like a very small shelter and I'm not sure it has water. I reported the noise to code but I also have a welfare concern for the animal. Is there a health or animal control avenue for this? — {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, animal welfare complaints are handled jointly by our health office and Cook County Animal Control. I'm forwarding your concern today. An inspector will follow up within {DAYS} business days. Thank you for looking out for the animal.

subject_tmpl: Graffiti keeps coming back — {STREET} alley
body_tmpl: Mayor, the alley on the east side of {STREET} between {CROSS} and {NUM} has been tagged repeatedly. The Village removes it and within a week it's back. This has happened at least four times this year. I appreciate that it's being removed, but clearly the underlying issue isn't being addressed. Is there any plan for cameras, lighting, or other deterrents in that corridor? — {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}

subject_tmpl: Construction noise starting before 7am at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: There is a remodeling project happening at {ADDRESS} on {STREET} and the contractor has been showing up and running power tools as early as 5:45 in the morning. I'm pretty sure that violates the Village noise ordinance. Today was the third time in two weeks. Can someone look into what hours are actually permitted? I work from home and this is extremely disruptive. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, construction work in residential zones is permitted between 7:00 a.m. and 8:00 p.m. on weekdays. We'll contact the property owner and the contractor immediately. If it happens again before 7 a.m., please call non-emergency police so it can be documented in real time.

subject_tmpl: Alley light out behind {STREET} — safety concern
body_tmpl: Hi there. The alley light behind my house on {STREET} (between {CROSS} and the next block south) went out about {DAYS} weeks ago. The alley is used by a lot of us for trash and recycling pickup and it is pitch black back there at night. A neighbor says someone tried to break into a garage last week — hard to say if it's related, but the darkness certainly doesn't help. Please prioritize this one. Thanks, {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}.

subject_tmpl: Response I received from {DEPT} was unhelpful
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo — I want to bring something to your attention. I contacted the {DEPT} on {DATE} about a problem at {ADDRESS} and the person I spoke with was dismissive and told me to 'just call 311.' That's not good enough. I pay taxes here and I expect a more professional response. The original issue is still unresolved. I'm copying you because I don't know who else to escalate to. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, I'm sorry to hear about your experience. That's not how we should be treating residents. I'll personally follow up with {DEPT} leadership and someone will contact you by {DATE} with a real update on your concern at {ADDRESS}. Thank you for bringing this to my attention.

subject_tmpl: Garbage and debris dumped on {STREET} near {CROSS}
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} — I live on {STREET} and someone has dumped a large pile of garbage — bags, old furniture, what looks like construction waste — near the curb at {CROSS}. It's been sitting there since at least {DATE}. The smell is starting and I'm worried about rodents. I'd very much appreciate a quick removal. Thank you.

subject_tmpl: Suspected drug activity at {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: I'm hesitant to put my name on this but I feel I need to say something. There is unusual traffic at {ADDRESS} on {STREET} at all hours — cars pulling up, someone coming to the door for less than a minute, then leaving. This happens day and night, including after midnight. I have {AGE}-year-old kids in the house and I'm worried. Is there a way to flag this for patrol without making a formal report with my name attached? You can reach me at {PHONE} if you need to follow up privately. — {FIRST}

subject_tmpl: Village ignored my complaint about {STREET} for {DAYS} days
body_tmpl: I am at my wit's end. I first reported an issue on {STREET} on {DATE}. I've called three times, sent two emails, and visited Village Hall in person. Each time I'm told it's 'in the queue.' It has now been {DAYS} days and nothing has happened. I'm a taxpayer and a homeowner and I deserve better than this. Mayor, I'm asking you directly: what is the process for actually getting something done in this village? — {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}, {PHONE}
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, I hear your frustration and it is completely valid. I've asked my assistant to pull every record tied to {ADDRESS} and {STREET} and have the accountable department head call you today. I am sorry this fell through — we will make it right.

subject_tmpl: Rat sighting near {ADDRESS} — need help
body_tmpl: Hello. I spotted what was clearly a large rat in the alley behind {ADDRESS} on {STREET} on {DATE}. This is not the first time — I've seen evidence of rodents for a few weeks but this is the first time I saw one in the open in daylight. There are several homes on this block and I'm wondering if there is a Village program for baiting alleys or if there's something specific I should do. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, rodent activity in that corridor is on our radar. I'll schedule an inspection of the alley near {ADDRESS} and coordinate baiting with our contracted exterminator. In the meantime, make sure all garbage lids are fully closed and food waste is bagged tightly. We'll be in touch within {DAYS} business days.

subject_tmpl: Neighbor at {ADDRESS} burning trash in backyard
body_tmpl: I'd like to report that my neighbor at {ADDRESS} on {STREET} has been burning trash and what smells like plastic in their backyard fire pit on multiple evenings this week. The smoke comes directly into my yard and into open windows. I have asthma and it's genuinely affecting my health. Is open burning permitted in Bellwood? I suspect not, but I don't know how to get this enforced. — {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Graffiti on {STREET} park shelter still not removed
body_tmpl: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, graffiti appeared on the park shelter at {STREET} and {CROSS} on approximately {DATE}. It has now been {DAYS} days. I reported it through the Village website but still haven't seen any action. My kids use that park and the content of the graffiti is inappropriate for children. Please push someone to take care of this. — {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}
reply_tmpl: Thank you for following up, {FIRST}. I've contacted Parks and Public Works directly — a crew will be out by {DATE}. I apologize that the website submission didn't generate faster action. We're working on improving that system.

subject_tmpl: Persistent barking dog at {ADDRESS} — already filed {CASE}
body_tmpl: I filed complaint {CASE} about a barking dog at {ADDRESS} roughly {DAYS} days ago. I received a letter saying the owner was warned. The situation has not improved at all — in fact last night was the worst yet, from about midnight to 3 a.m. I understand there's a process but I need to know: what happens after the first warning? Is there a fine? An escalation path? I'm losing sleep and I can't keep absorbing this indefinitely. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, I understand this is ongoing and I'm sorry for the continued disruption. After an initial warning, a second documented complaint triggers a formal notice of violation, which carries fines starting at {AMOUNT}. I've updated case {CASE} with your new report. An officer will verify the situation within {DAYS} days.

subject_tmpl: Light pole at {ADDRESS} leaning — potential hazard
body_tmpl: I noticed that the light pole on {STREET} near {ADDRESS} is leaning at a noticeable angle — maybe 15 to 20 degrees. I don't know if it was hit by a vehicle or if the base is rotting, but it looks unstable. If it falls it could hit cars or pedestrians. Wanted to flag this as soon as possible. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, thank you for the heads up — a leaning pole is a safety issue we'll treat as urgent. I'm dispatching someone today to assess the pole at {ADDRESS}. If it poses immediate danger we'll rope off the area and notify ComEd for emergency repair.

subject_tmpl: Fed up with noisy neighbors — Village hasn't helped
body_tmpl: Mayor, I've been dealing with noise from the property next door at {ADDRESS} for months. I've contacted code enforcement, the police non-emergency line, and even came in person to Village Hall. Each department tells me to contact a different one. Nobody takes ownership. Meanwhile I'm losing sleep night after night. I'm a single parent with two children and I'm at my limit. I voted for you because I believed you cared about quality of life in Bellwood. Please show me I was right. — {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}

subject_tmpl: Semi-truck idling all night on {STREET}
body_tmpl: There is a commercial semi-truck that has been parking on {STREET} near {CROSS} and idling for hours at night. The diesel smell gets into my house and the engine noise makes it impossible to sleep with windows open. This happened three nights this week. I don't think commercial trucks are supposed to be on residential streets overnight — can someone check the ordinance and deal with this? {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}.

subject_tmpl: Possible rodent infestation spreading from {ADDRESS}
body_tmpl: Priya or whoever handles rodent complaints — I believe a vacant property at {ADDRESS} on {STREET} is harboring a rodent infestation that is spreading to neighboring homes including mine. I've seen rats in my garage and a neighbor two doors down said the same thing. The vacant lot has tall weeds, old appliances, and bags of garbage that have been there all season. Can a health inspector visit and coordinate cleanup? — {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}
reply_tmpl: Hello {FIRST}, this is exactly the kind of source issue we need to address. I'll schedule an inspection of {ADDRESS} within {DAYS} business days and coordinate with Code Enforcement on the debris and vegetation. Rodent bait stations will be placed in the alley as well. Thank you for the detailed information.

subject_tmpl: Street on my block darker than the rest of Bellwood
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I've noticed that {STREET} between {CROSS} and {NUM} is significantly darker than surrounding streets after dark. I think there may be a lighting gap — these poles may not have been updated or may be on a different circuit. Compared to Washington Blvd it's like a different world. I know it sounds like a small thing but lighting affects safety and property values. Is there any capital plan that includes {STREET}? — {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Reckless driving and loud music from vehicles on {STREET}
body_tmpl: Officer Banks — I wanted to document something that happens nearly every weekend on {STREET} around {TIME}. Groups of cars, sometimes {NUM} or more, park near {CROSS} and play music at concert volume, rev engines, and occasionally do burnouts. It's a neighborhood with families and elderly residents. Can we get some targeted enforcement during those hours? {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, I've noted the time and location. I'll add {STREET} near {CROSS} to the enhanced weekend patrol rotation. If you can text {PHONE} while it's happening in real time, I can dispatch immediately. Thank you for the detailed report.

subject_tmpl: Neighbor's rooster waking the whole block at dawn
body_tmpl: I'll be honest — I didn't think I'd ever send an email like this, but here we are. The house at {ADDRESS} appears to have a rooster, or possibly multiple roosters, that begin crowing well before sunrise every single morning. I'm at {ADDRESS}, just two houses away, and I can hear it clearly through closed windows. I have to assume keeping livestock in a residential zone is against code. Can someone look into this? — {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Sidewalk and lighting both bad on {STREET} — combined hazard
body_tmpl: Mayor, I want to bring to your attention that {STREET} between {CROSS} and {NUM} has two overlapping problems: broken/uneven sidewalk panels and non-functioning streetlights. On their own either would be manageable, but together they create a real fall hazard, especially for seniors and anyone with mobility issues. I've reported the sidewalk to Public Works and the light through the Village website but I haven't seen any action on either. Could both be addressed together? — {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, you've raised a good point about coordinating repairs rather than addressing them piecemeal. I'm asking Public Works and our engineer to do a joint walk of that block and develop a combined fix. Expect contact within the week. — Mayor Okonkwo

subject_tmpl: Graffiti on garage door facing alley — {STREET}
body_tmpl: Hello, graffiti was sprayed on the garage door of my property at {ADDRESS} on {STREET} overnight on {DATE}. It's visible from the alley. I have photos if they'd be helpful. I'd like to know: is there a Village program that helps with removal costs, or am I on my own to clean it up? And should I be filing a police report as well? — {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, yes, please file a police report for the record — you can do that by calling the non-emergency line. The Village does offer a graffiti abatement program for property owners; email me your photos and address and I can get you enrolled. There's no cost for the first incident.

subject_tmpl: Nothing ever changes on {STREET} — long-term resident gripe
body_tmpl: Mayor, I have lived on {STREET} for {DAYS} years. In that time I've seen the same potholes patched and re-patched, the same lights go out and eventually get fixed, the same graffiti appear and eventually get cleaned. Nothing ever seems to get solved permanently. I'm not blaming you personally but I feel like the Village operates in reaction mode rather than planning ahead. Is there any proactive maintenance plan for neighborhoods like mine? I'd genuinely like to know. {FIRST} {LAST}

subject_tmpl: Holiday decorations removed light from {CROSS} pole
body_tmpl: I think when the holiday light installation crew was working on {CROSS} in late {DATE} they may have accidentally disconnected or damaged the regular streetlight on the pole near {ADDRESS}. The light was fine before the holiday work and has been out ever since. I've noticed it's just that one pole while the others on the block are fine. Could someone check whether the holiday installation is related? — {FIRST} {LAST}
reply_tmpl: Good catch, {FIRST}. We'll have a crew inspect the pole at {ADDRESS} and {CROSS} this week to see if the holiday installation affected the fixture. Thank you for the context — it really helps us diagnose faster.

subject_tmpl: Food smell and possible pest issue from neighbor's property
body_tmpl: Hi, I'm writing about the property at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. There is a persistent strong odor — food waste, I think — coming from the yard, especially on warm days. I've also seen evidence of rodents near my fence that borders that property. I don't want to cause trouble for my neighbors but this is a health issue. Can a health officer do a quiet inspection? My name is {FIRST} {LAST} and I'd prefer my complaint stay confidential if possible. {PHONE}

subject_tmpl: Staff rudeness when I called about my complaint
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm writing to you because I had a very unpleasant experience when I called Village Hall on {DATE} to follow up on complaint {CASE}. The staff member I reached — I didn't get their name — was dismissive, interrupted me several times, and actually hung up before I finished speaking. I'm not calling to get anyone fired, but that kind of behavior reflects poorly on Bellwood. I'm happy to describe the call in more detail if it's helpful. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, this is deeply concerning and I want you to know I take it seriously. I've shared the date and time with my office manager to identify the call and we will follow up internally. Please don't hesitate to contact my assistant Tina directly at any time. You deserve to be treated with respect. — Mayor Okonkwo

subject_tmpl: Abandoned vehicle on {STREET} for over {DAYS} days
body_tmpl: {FIRST} {LAST} here, calling attention to an abandoned vehicle parked on {STREET} near {CROSS}. It has flat tires, no plates, and has not moved in over {DAYS} days. It's taking up a parking space on a block where parking is already tight, and honestly it's just an eyesore. Is there a process to have it towed? {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, we can place a 72-hour notice on a vehicle and arrange towing if it hasn't moved afterward. I'll notify our liaison with the police parking unit to tag the vehicle at {STREET} and {CROSS}. Should be resolved within the week.

subject_tmpl: Streetlight report — {NUM} block of {STREET}, pole near fire hydrant
body_tmpl: Quick report: the light on the pole closest to the fire hydrant on the {NUM} block of {STREET} has been out since at least {DATE}. I use this as a landmark when giving people directions to my house so I noticed right away when it went dark. Reporting it here since I wasn't sure whether to call or email. {FIRST} {LAST}.

subject_tmpl: Why does {STREET} never get repaved while others do?
body_tmpl: Mayor Okonkwo, I have a straightforward question: {STREET} has not been repaved or had significant maintenance in as long as I can remember, while I've seen crews work on {CROSS} and other streets repeatedly. How are streets selected for maintenance? Is there a schedule that residents can see? I want to understand the criteria rather than just feel like my block is being ignored. Thank you. {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}.
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, that's a fair question. We use a Pavement Condition Index rating system to prioritize streets. I'd like to have our engineer send you the current ratings for {STREET} and surrounding blocks so you can see where it stands. Thank you for asking constructively. — Mayor Okonkwo

subject_tmpl: Neighbor's outdoor speakers too loud every evening
body_tmpl: I'm reaching out about a noise issue with my neighbor at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}. They have outdoor Bluetooth speakers and use them loudly every single evening from about {TIME} until sometimes 11 p.m. or later. They're otherwise good neighbors and I feel awkward confronting them directly. I'd like the Village to send a general notice about the noise ordinance so I'm not singled out. Is that something you can do? {FIRST} {LAST}.

subject_tmpl: General gripe — Bellwood needs more neighborhood investment
body_tmpl: Mayor, I'll keep this short. My neighborhood near {STREET} and {CROSS} feels neglected compared to areas closer to Village Hall. Lights go out and take forever to fix. Alleys are littered. Code violations sit unaddressed. I don't think this is about lack of resources — I think it's about priorities. I'm asking you to make neighborhoods like mine a higher priority. A lot of working families live here and we care about where we live. — {FIRST} {LAST}, {ADDRESS}
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, your message hit home. Geographic equity in service delivery is something I think about a lot. I'm scheduling a walkthrough of the {STREET} and {CROSS} area with department heads in the next two weeks. I'd welcome you to join us if you're available. — Mayor Okonkwo

subject_tmpl: Fireworks being set off nightly near {STREET} and {CROSS}
body_tmpl: I need to report that someone in the vicinity of {STREET} and {CROSS} has been setting off what sound like large fireworks almost every night around {TIME}. This has been going on for {DAYS} days — well outside of any holiday. My dog is terrified and my {AGE}-year-old keeps waking up frightened. I also worry about fire risk given how dry it's been. Can police or code enforcement do something about this? — {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}

subject_tmpl: Dead animal smell near {ADDRESS} — possible decomposition in vacant lot
body_tmpl: There is a very strong odor near {ADDRESS} on {STREET} that I believe is a decomposing animal in the vacant lot on the corner. It's been getting worse for about a week. I can't locate the source from outside the fence. Can a health officer or public works crew check the lot and remove whatever is causing the smell? I have elderly neighbors who find it very unpleasant. {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Thank you for reporting this, {FIRST}. I'll send someone to inspect the vacant lot at {ADDRESS} on {STREET} today. If it's a deceased animal we'll arrange removal through Cook County or our public works crew depending on the situation.

subject_tmpl: Prior Village response didn't fix my problem — {CASE}
body_tmpl: Mayor — I received what appeared to be a resolution notice for case {CASE} (barking dog at {ADDRESS} on {STREET}), but the problem continues. The letter said the owner was informed and the matter was closed. Nobody asked me whether the situation improved before closing it. The dog was barking again within 48 hours of the notice. Closing a case without checking back with the complainant seems like a flawed process. Can the case be reopened? {FIRST} {LAST}, {PHONE}.
reply_tmpl: Dear {FIRST}, you're right — cases should not be closed without confirming resolution. I've asked Code to reopen {CASE} and we'll add a required follow-up step for noise complaints going forward. Someone will contact you within {DAYS} business days to confirm the current status.

subject_tmpl: Streetlight on {STREET} replaced but still not working
body_tmpl: Hi Frank or whoever — I reported a light out on {STREET} near {ADDRESS} back in {DATE}. I saw a crew out there last week and assumed it was being repaired. But the light is still not turning on at night. Not sure if the bulb was replaced but the wiring wasn't checked, or what happened. Can someone go back and verify the repair was actually completed? Thanks, {FIRST} {LAST}.
reply_tmpl: Hi {FIRST}, that's frustrating — thanks for closing the loop with us. I'll send a crew back to verify the repair and test the fixture after dark if needed. We'll get it confirmed by {DATE}.
